message:
test(cli): add stdin/interactive testing capability to runCli

Extends the test harness to support simulating user input via stdin,
enabling comprehensive testing of CLI interactive prompts.

Changes to runCli():
- Add optional `stdin` parameter to pass input strings
- Conditionally pipe stdin when provided (vs "ignore" mode)
- Write stdin lines with 50ms delays for readline compatibility
- Initial 300ms delay before first write to allow prompt rendering

New test cases for `test execute --interactive`:
1. Accepts stdin input for prompts - validates interactive flow:
   - Observed result, potency check, confidence level inputs
   - Optional fields (executed by, notes)
   - Decline to apply suggestions (answer 'n')
   - Verifies test completion without hypothesis state changes

2. With apply transitions hypotheses - validates full workflow:
   - Same interactive inputs but accepts suggestion application
   - Verifies hypothesis state transitions (confirmed/refuted)
   - Validates notes are properly captured in execution record

These tests exercise the readline-based interactive prompting system
added in the test execute command, ensuring the CLI correctly handles
multi-step user dialogs and integrates with the suggestion engine.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
